selection.status_reason: Live thesis monitor refreshed the active weekly pick.

reasons: Price trend remains constructive: 5-day momentum is 7.2% and 20-day momentum is 14.6%.
Trend quality is supported by 60% positive sessions over the last 10 trading days and the price sitting 7.8% relative to its 20-day average.
Risk profile shows daily volatility around 1.6%, downside volatility near 1.2%, and a recent max drawdown of 5.2%.
Volume confirmation is 1.3% versus the trailing baseline, which helps separate steady breakouts from thin momentum.
Relative strength is 5.3% versus SPY over 5 days and 2.5% versus the sector ETF.
News signal is neutral with score 0.57, confidence 0.63, and 0.2 weighted articles across 3 sources.
GPT-5 article classification and company-news provider coverage rated the news flow neutral at 0.57, after adjusting for relevance, recency, source quality, and story concentration.
Evidence quality: 3 articles across 3 sources, 0.2 effective weighted articles, average relevance 0.74, average source quality 0.82, confidence 0.63.
Technical weights were calibrated on 2303 historical symbol-weeks against 5-day excess return (IC 0.02).
Overall model score is 0.144 with medium confidence.

news_evidence.llm_reason: Directly about Johnson & Johnson and its investor appeal; however it reads as a trending/primer piece rather than new material news (no reported earnings, FDA actions, or M&A), so likely limited short-term market-moving content.
